query: I was sorting through some cleaning supplies earlier and it felt a bit stuffy in the utility room how is the air quality in there right now

steps.thought: The user wants to know the air quality in the utility room. I can use get_room_states to check current environmental states including PM10 (air quality). First, I need the room_id for the utility room. I'll fetch the rooms list.
I have the utility_room id. Now I'll get its environmental states to check air quality (PM10).
The utility room has a PM10 reading of 42.59 μg/m³, which is considered good air quality (typically under 50 is good). The user felt stuffy but the measured air quality is fine.

action_input.answer: The air quality in the utility room is actually quite good right now. The PM10 level is 42.59 μg/m³, which is well within healthy limits. It might have felt stuffy due to other factors, but the air quality sensor shows clean air.
